capability_gaps: No living literature ingestion or claim extraction.
No formal problem ontology or reduction search.
No proof-obligation gate before claiming speedups.
No representation-theory toolkit for nonabelian HSP/coset states.
No hidden-shift phase-state or sieve simulator.
No hard instance generators for GI/code equivalence/lattice families.
No counterexample search or theorem-proving path.
No experiment database with scaling-law model comparison.

core_assumptions: There exist structured instance families where collective observables have polynomial descriptions.
Those observables are not equivalent to classical canonicalization or refinement.
falsifiers: Coset-state distinguishability remains exponentially small under all low-complexity observables tested.
Classical GI/code-equivalence tools solve every generated family.
likely_to_fail_because: Known Fourier-sampling barriers are strong and old.
Collective measurements may require exponential entanglement or preprocessing.
Hard instances may have too little representation-theoretic signal.
critique_passes.revised_recommendation: Keep as high-upside, but only with no-go-aware measurement search and hard-instance generators.
critique_passes.target: Nonabelian HSP / code equivalence / graph isomorphism

core_assumptions: There are algebraic hidden-shift distributions with phase-state structure not captured by generic subset-sum hardness.
A sieve improvement can be linked to a meaningful lattice or number-theory problem.
falsifiers: All families either have simple classical correlation attacks or reduce to generic Kuperberg/Regev scaling.
No reversible map with polynomial precision survives cost audit.
likely_to_fail_because: Generic DHSP has resisted polynomial algorithms for decades.
Structured exceptions may be classically easy.
Approximate lattice periodicity may need infeasible precision.
critique_passes.revised_recommendation: Keep as top target; add Gowers/derivative tests and phase-sieve simulator before broader speculation.
critique_passes.target: Hidden shift / dihedral HSP / lattice route

core_assumptions: The generated witness has a recursively describable structure.
The query model has a plausible explicit computational analogue.
falsifiers: Witness size improvements vanish outside handcrafted Boolean functions.
No reduction connects the query family to a natural problem.
likely_to_fail_because: It may produce only oracle/query separations.
SDP witnesses can be opaque and fail to generalize.
critique_passes.revised_recommendation: Build it, but force every witness through natural-family and reduction gates.

core_assumptions: There are structured operators whose spectra are quantum-accessible but classically hard to estimate.
Encoding costs remain below the claimed separation.
falsifiers: Classical trace/Lanczos/sketching recovers the same invariant.
likely_to_fail_because: Most QSVT novelty is incremental or input-model-sensitive.
Classical randomized linear algebra may dequantize the advantage.
critique_passes.revised_recommendation: Keep as secondary target with strict cost calculus and dequantization checks.

core_assumptions: LLMs are constrained by typed schemas, literature citations, proof obligations, and adversarial review.
Generated leads are evaluated by executable structural tests.
falsifiers: Generated ideas are not traceable to mechanisms or falsifiers.
The same dead-end families recur with new names.
likely_to_fail_because: LLMs hallucinate complexity claims and overfit labels like HSP/QFT.
The old corpus proves prompt novelty is not research novelty.
critique_passes.revised_recommendation: Use LLMs only as breadth generators inside a hard-gated research pipeline.

blunt_diagnosis: The old loop is not a credible path to a Shor-level algorithm.
It searches tiny circuits and arbitrary oracle puzzles, then lets an LLM label complexity from N<=3 data.
Most successful runs rediscover Bernstein-Vazirani-like parity structure; most exotic names are unsolved or unsupported.
The dashboard's 'solved novel algorithms' criterion is just success_rate >= 1.0 on tiny instances, not a research result.
The project needs explicit scalable families, classical baselines, structural quantum handles, and kill criteria.

mission: Maximize expected contribution to a Shor-level quantum algorithmic breakthrough.

module_decisions.reason: Depth-5/N<=3 circuit search optimizes the wrong object.
Only wraps the toy circuit search.
Prompt-driven toy-problem generation caused the bad result corpus.
module_decisions.replacement: LLM hypothesis generator gated by literature/proof obligations

module_decisions.reason: LLM complexity labels from tiny data are actively misleading.
module_decisions.replacement: proof obligation gate plus baseline checker

module_decisions.reason: Qiskit snippets are not research contributions.
module_decisions.replacement: algorithm-schema synthesis and formal proof artifacts

module_decisions.reason: A timed loop creates volume, not insight.
Dashboard polish rewards demos and legacy hits.
module_decisions.replacement: research log generated from audit artifacts

module_decisions.reason: The corpus is useful mainly as evidence of failure modes.
Vendored 500MB toolchain has no research value.
module_decisions.replacement: document external setup if deployment is ever needed

module_decisions.reason: Good first agenda generator but still too static.

module_decisions.replacement: add group representation, Gowers, span-program, and lattice tests

new_architecture.1_literature_memory: Continuously ingest papers, extract mechanisms/barriers/reductions, and update a knowledge graph.
new_architecture.2_problem_ontology: Represent problems, reductions, no-go barriers, and hard families as graph data.
new_architecture.3_hypothesis_factory: Generate candidates by recombining mechanisms across the ontology, not by inventing oracles.
new_architecture.5_structural_labs: Run dedicated experiments for HSP/coset states, hidden shift, span programs, walks, QSVT, and Gowers structure.
new_architecture.6_counterexample_loop: Automatically search small finite models for falsifiers before investing in proof.
new_architecture.7_human_review: Produce decision logs and questions for expert review instead of dashboard trophies.

proof_obligations.obligation: Define an explicit asymptotic problem family.
proof_obligations.why_required: Shor-level claims are asymptotic, not small-instance phenomena.
Many quantum speedups disappear under realistic data access.

proof_obligations.obligation: Name the best known classical algorithms and hardness evidence.
proof_obligations.why_required: Beating brute force is not meaningful.

proof_obligations.obligation: Connect the family to a known hard problem, lower-bound target, or frontier reduction.
proof_obligations.why_required: This separates natural problems from arbitrary oracle puzzles.

proof_obligations.obligation: Identify the quantum mechanism: Fourier, phase estimation, walk, QSVT, span program, collective measurement, etc.
proof_obligations.why_required: The mechanism drives both search and proof.

proof_obligations.obligation: Account for state preparation, block encoding, QRAM, precision, and reversible arithmetic.
proof_obligations.why_required: Hidden costs often erase the claimed speedup.

proof_obligations.obligation: Specify the measurement and decoding procedure.
proof_obligations.why_required: For nonabelian/coset problems this is usually the hard part.

proof_obligations.obligation: State a success probability theorem or conjecture with parameters.
proof_obligations.why_required: Finite experiments need a proof target.

proof_obligations.obligation: Separate query, gate, space, precision, and classical postprocessing complexity.
proof_obligations.why_required: A query speedup alone may not be an algorithmic speedup.

proof_obligations.obligation: List applicable no-go barriers and how the proposal avoids them.
Check whether a classical randomized/low-rank/dequantized method matches the claimed advantage.
proof_obligations.why_required: Modern quantum linear algebra claims especially need this.

proof_obligations.obligation: Provide explicit experiments or counterexamples that would kill the idea.
proof_obligations.why_required: A research engine must conserve attention.

falsifying_evidence: New leads are not traceable to papers or barriers.
Researchers cannot query for analogous proof techniques across domains.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Seeded by literature_radar.py; full parser/search still needed.
likely_failure_modes: Extraction summarizes papers without capturing proof constraints.
The graph becomes a bibliography instead of a source of hypotheses.
ranked_improvements.title: Living literature knowledge graph with technique extraction
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Breakthrough work is usually recombination of known mechanisms and unresolved barriers; the project needs memory of the field.

falsifying_evidence: Candidates pass without input model, lower-bound target, or oracle/state-preparation costs.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Basic enforcement implemented in proof_gate.py; candidate generation still needs to call it by default.
likely_failure_modes: The gate becomes paperwork and blocks speculative ideas too early.
ranked_improvements.title: Proof-obligation gate for every candidate algorithm
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Prevents the system from recording unsupported speedup claims and forces scalable problem families, reductions, and cost accounting.

falsifying_evidence: A proposed lead can ignore known no-go barriers without being flagged.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Implemented in problem_ontology.py with first reduction/barrier graph.
likely_failure_modes: Ontology freezes current beliefs and misses unconventional routes.
Edges are too informal for automated reasoning.
ranked_improvements.title: Problem and reduction ontology with no-go barriers
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Shor-level algorithms come from reductions and structural reframings; a circuit search cannot see that.

falsifying_evidence: All observables collapse to strong Fourier sampling or classical refinement invariants.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not yet implemented beyond coset fingerprint smoke metrics.
likely_failure_modes: Interesting measurements require exponential classical preprocessing.
ranked_improvements.title: Representation-theory and coset-state laboratory
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Nonabelian HSP, GI, and code equivalence require irreps, coset states, and collective measurements, not qubit-level BFS.

falsifying_evidence: Sieve scaling matches Kuperberg/Regev with no structural improvement.
A classical correlation attack recovers the shift.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Partially supported by hidden_shift_metrics and new Gowers/derivative tests.
ranked_improvements.title: Hidden-shift and dihedral phase-state sieve workbench
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: This is one of the few frontier zones with a known subexponential quantum algorithm and lattice implications.

falsifying_evidence: Witnesses do not generalize across n.
No explicit problem family survives the proof-obligation gate.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented; should replace most oracle-circuit search.
likely_failure_modes: SDPs scale poorly and yield uninterpretable witnesses.
ranked_improvements.title: Adversary-bound SDP and span-program synthesizer
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Span programs convert query lower-bound structure into algorithms; this is a principled alternative to random circuit search.

falsifying_evidence: Conjectures lack formal predicates.
No generated counterexample changes a research lead.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Counterexamples do not scale or teach structure.
ranked_improvements.title: Conjecture and counterexample engine
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Research progress often comes from killing false conjectures cheaply and finding the exact boundary of a phenomenon.

falsifying_evidence: No polynomial-bond ansatz separates hard instances.
Success relies on memorizing small instances.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Optimized measurement has no clean mathematical description.
ranked_improvements.title: Tensor-network search for multi-register measurements
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Known barriers suggest single-register sampling is too weak; tensor networks are the plausible classical tool for exploring collective measurements.

falsifying_evidence: A claimed speedup survives only against brute force.
Classical randomized estimators match the proposed quantum invariant.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Specified in proof obligations; checker not yet implemented.
likely_failure_modes: The checker rejects speculative leads without proof.
ranked_improvements.title: Classical baseline and dequantization checker
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Most false quantum speedups hide in weak baselines, data loading, or low-rank dequantization.

falsifying_evidence: Derivative spectra are sparse only for classically learnable families.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Implemented exact small-instance U^k and derivative spectrum metrics.
likely_failure_modes: High Gowers norm correlates with classical learnability.
ranked_improvements.title: Higher-order Fourier and Gowers-uniformity testbed
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Large-Gowers-norm hidden shift algorithms show that higher-order harmonic analysis can be a real algorithmic handle.

falsifying_evidence: Period ridges require exponential precision.
No reduction to a hard lattice problem is found.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Classical lattice reduction exploits the same structure.
ranked_improvements.title: Lattice and algebraic-number-theory periodicity workbench
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Hallgren/Regev-style ideas show hidden periodicity in number theory is one of the best places to hunt.

falsifying_evidence: No candidate invariant survives data-loading and dequantization checks.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Focus drifts to incremental primitive optimization.
ranked_improvements.title: QSVT/block-encoding cost calculus
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: QSVT is powerful but dangerous: many apparent speedups disappear once encoding and precision are counted.

falsifying_evidence: Generated analogies cannot state proof obligations or falsifiers.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
ranked_improvements.title: Cross-paper technique recombination engine
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: The system should ask questions like: can the proof trick from hidden shift apply to code equivalence?

falsifying_evidence: No nontrivial reduction can be machine-checked on finite models.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Formalizing domains costs more than the search.
Generated reductions are too weak or circular.
ranked_improvements.title: Reduction miner using symbolic unification
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: A new reduction can be as valuable as a new circuit; reductions define what a breakthrough would affect.

falsifying_evidence: The engine scores toy BV variants higher than known frontier problems.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Partially implemented through audit diagnostics; benchmark suite still needed.
ranked_improvements.title: Benchmark suite of known algorithms and no-go barriers
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: A discovery engine must rediscover Shor/Simon/Hallgren mechanisms and fail on known-dead strategies before being trusted.

falsifying_evidence: Claims are not linked to papers, reductions, or experiments.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented; old Theorist/Analyzer should be deleted rather than extended.
likely_failure_modes: Critique is rhetorical rather than mathematical.
ranked_improvements.title: Multi-agent hypothesis generation with adversarial review
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: LLMs are useful for breadth, but only with red-team critique and hard gates.

falsifying_evidence: Synthesized schema lacks a proof path or scalable invariant.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
ranked_improvements.title: Program synthesis over algorithm schemas, not gate sequences
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Searching over QFT/phase-estimation/walk/QSVT schemas has far higher ceiling than raw gates.

falsifying_evidence: Positive signals disappear under larger generated families.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Wrong model class gives false optimism.
ranked_improvements.title: Scaling-law experiment manager with Bayesian model comparison
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Finite experiments matter only if they compare asymptotic hypotheses and fail fast.

falsifying_evidence: Only trivial circuits can be verified.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Proof assistant libraries lack required mathematics.
ranked_improvements.title: Formal verification path for candidate algorithms
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: A serious system should eventually prove circuit identities and reductions, not trust prose.

falsifying_evidence: Gap-overlap product never beats amplitude amplification.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Basic spectral metric exists; full analyzer not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Spectral proxies do not imply implementable walks.
ranked_improvements.title: Quantum-walk geometry analyzer
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Walk algorithms need geometry; this checks whether state spaces beat Grover before algorithm design.

falsifying_evidence: No family passes classical-baseline and proof-obligation gates.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Generated structures have no hard baseline.
ranked_improvements.title: Finite-field and algebraic-geometry family generator
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Explicit families are the raw material for hidden shift, property testing, and code-equivalence experiments.

falsifying_evidence: Classical canonicalization solves all generated families quickly.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Generated instances are easy for classical tools.
ranked_improvements.title: Hard instance generators for code equivalence and graph isomorphism
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Without hard families, coset-state experiments are meaningless.

falsifying_evidence: Any future run produces unsupported speedup claims again.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Recommended; root entrypoint already bypasses it, files still present for now.
likely_failure_modes: Deletion hides historical mistakes instead of documenting them.
ranked_improvements.title: Delete the old tiny-circuit LLM loop
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: It actively creates false confidence and should not remain a first-class path.

falsifying_evidence: Dashboard presents them as successes.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Partially done through dashboard relabeling; physical quarantine still pending.
likely_failure_modes: Historical data may be accidentally treated as discoveries.
ranked_improvements.title: Quarantine legacy result corpus as negative examples
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: The old outputs are useful only as anti-examples for the proof gate.

falsifying_evidence: Generated code still controls evaluation without typed semantics.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented; best resolved by deleting legacy simulator.
likely_failure_modes: Better answer is to delete the code path.
ranked_improvements.title: Sandbox or eliminate generated Python execution
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: The current `exec` path is unsafe and scientifically noisy.

falsifying_evidence: Dashboard metrics drive decisions instead of proof obligations.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Recommended; wording changed but file remains.
likely_failure_modes: A research log still needs a readable surface.
ranked_improvements.title: Remove web dashboard as a central artifact
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: The dashboard incentivizes demos and status optics instead of research progress.

falsifying_evidence: Rejected ideas keep returning without new evidence.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Reviews are not tied to experiments.
ranked_improvements.title: Expert review workflow with decision logs
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Breakthrough odds rise when machine-generated hypotheses are aggressively reviewed by humans with field context.

falsifying_evidence: The same toy-oracle or no-go-barrier mistake recurs.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Partially represented by ontology barriers.
likely_failure_modes: Negative results overgeneralize and block creativity.
ranked_improvements.title: Negative-result database
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Avoiding repeated dead ends is a direct expected-value gain.

falsifying_evidence: New relevant papers are absent from the knowledge graph for months.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Static seeds implemented; recurring automation not implemented.
likely_failure_modes: Noise from irrelevant papers overwhelms signal.
ranked_improvements.title: Recurring arXiv monitor for quantum algorithms
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Current literature can change frontier priorities; the project should notice automatically.

falsifying_evidence: Classical trace estimators match quantum access-model cost.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
ranked_improvements.title: Random-matrix and spectral invariant search
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: May reveal separations for isomorphism-like problems, but many invariants are classically estimable.

falsifying_evidence: Used to rank ideas before proof obligations are met.
ranked_improvements.implementation_status: Not implemented.
ranked_improvements.title: Fault-tolerant resource estimator
ranked_improvements.why_it_matters: Useful after a real candidate exists; not a primary discovery driver.
